summary: Replayed ADR 0278 from the merged mainline after release 0.177.92 landed on `origin/main`, fixed the Docker runtime nftables persistence bug in commit `e1b0ceb64999621f468bb85d1c21964cfcc6b4ca`, re-ran the governed Gotenberg live apply successfully from that exact main commit, and captured fresh guest-local plus authenticated gateway PDF-render proofs before promoting the canonical platform version bump.

verification.check: Remote pre-push gate on merged-main integration
verification.observed: Both `git push origin HEAD:main` runs for the ADR 0278 integration passed the full remote pre-push gate, including `agent-standards`, `alert-rule-validation`, `ansible-lint`, `ansible-syntax`, `artifact-secret-scan`, `dependency-direction`, `dependency-graph`, `documentation-index`, `generated-docs`, `generated-portals`, `integration-tests`, `packer-validate`, `policy-validation`, `schema-validation`, `security-scan`, `service-completeness`, `tofu-validate`, `type-check`, `workstream-surfaces`, and `yaml-lint`.

verification.check: First merged-main replay exposed the Docker runtime recovery bug
verification.observed: The first authoritative merged-main replay from commit `8704a9798` failed with `iptables: No chain/target/match by that name` while starting Gotenberg because the Docker runtime role reloaded the full nftables ruleset and temporarily flushed Docker-managed tables; the failing transcript is preserved in `receipts/live-applies/evidence/2026-03-30-ws-0278-merged-main-live-apply.txt`.

verification.check: Docker runtime replay fix on main
verification.observed: Commit `e1b0ceb64999621f468bb85d1c21964cfcc6b4ca` changed the Docker runtime role so it persists the forward-compat nftables patch without reloading the full ruleset, and the focused pytest slice for `tests/test_docker_runtime_role.py` plus `tests/test_gotenberg_runtime_role.py` passed from the exact-main integration worktree.

`ALLOW_IN_PLACE_MUTATION=true make live-apply-service service=gotenberg env=production EXTRA_ARGS='-e bypass_promotion=true'` completed successfully from source commit `e1b0ceb64999621f468bb85d1c21964cfcc6b4ca` with recap `docker-runtime-lv3 : ok=259 changed=112 unreachable=0 failed=0 skipped=29 rescued=0 ignored=0`, captured in `receipts/live-applies/evidence/2026-03-30-ws-0278-merged-main-live-apply-r2.txt`.

The guest-local health probe returned `{"status":"up"...}` from `http://127.0.0.1:3007/health`; the merged-main local Chromium render returned `pdf_magic=%PDF` and `pdf_bytes=31015`; and the merged-main local LibreOffice render returned `pdf_magic=%PDF` and `pdf_bytes=14041`, as captured in the `2026-03-30-ws-0278-merged-main-local-*` evidence files.

Using the controller-local platform-context bearer token, `https://api.lv3.org/v1/gotenberg/health` returned `{"status":"up"...}`, and the authenticated Chromium gateway render returned `pdf_magic=%PDF` with `pdf_bytes=32038`, as captured in the `2026-03-30-ws-0278-merged-main-gateway-*` evidence files.

notes: This receipt is the canonical merged-main replay that justifies bumping `platform_version` from `0.130.60` to `0.130.61` for ADR 0278.
The service remains intentionally private on `docker-runtime-lv3`; the supported authenticated operator path is the governed API gateway route `https://api.lv3.org/v1/gotenberg`.
The earlier branch-local receipt remains part of the audit trail, but the latest canonical Gotenberg live-apply evidence now points at the merged-main receipt recorded here.
